bullets: • Regularization is a technique used to prevent overfitting in machine learning models by adding a penalty term to the cost function that discourages large parameter values.
• The penalty term is typically added to the cost function as a squared term of the parameters, with a large coefficient that increases as the parameter value increases.
• By minimizing the cost function, the model will be forced to choose smaller parameter values, resulting in a simpler model that is less prone to overfitting.

• The value of the penalty coefficient, known as the regularization parameter (lambda), determines the relative importance of the penalty term and must be chosen carefully to balance the trade-off between model simplicity and fit to the training data.
• The goal of regularization is to find a balance between minimizing the mean squared error and keeping the parameters small, resulting in a model that generalizes well to new, unseen data.
raw:
• Regularization is a technique used to prevent overfitting in machine learning models by adding a penalty term to the cost function that discourages large parameter values.
• The penalty term is typically added to the cost function as a squared term of the parameters, with a large coefficient that increases as the parameter value increases.
• By minimizing the cost function, the model will be forced to choose smaller parameter values, resulting in a simpler model that is less prone to overfitting.
• The value of the penalty coefficient, known as the regularization parameter (lambda), determines the relative importance of the penalty term and must be chosen carefully to balance the trade-off between model simplicity and fit to the training data.
• The goal of regularization is to find a balance between minimizing the mean squared error and keeping the parameters small, resulting in a model that generalizes well to new, unseen data.
